items.title: JavaFX ToolTip
items.summary: JavaFX ToolTip is a small popup with explanatory text shown when a user hovers the mouse over a JavaFX control.

items.title: JavaFX ToolBar
items.summary: JavaFX ToolBar is a horizontal or vertical bar of buttons and other JavaFX components typically used to select or interact with different tools.

items.title: JavaFX FXML
items.summary: JavaFX FXML is an XML format used to compose JavaFX GUIs in a way similar to how you compose web GUIs with HTML.

items.title: JavaFX MenuBar
items.summary: The JavaFX MenuBar adds a top menu to a JavaFX application, just like you are used to from desktop applications.

items.title: Java Certificate
items.summary: The Java Certificate class represents a cryptographic identity certificate, possibly signed by a Certificate Authority (CA).

items.title: Java KeyPair
items.summary: The Java KeyPair class represents a public key, private key pair used in asymmetric encryption (e.g. RSA).

items.title: Java KeyPairGenerator
items.summary: The Java KeyPairGenerator can generate asymmetric encryption key pairs (public key, private key). This Java KeyPairGenerator tutorial explains how to use the KeyPairGenerator class.

items.title: Java KeyGenerator
items.summary: The Java KeyGenerator can generate symmetric encryption keys. This Java KeyGenerator tutorial explains how to use the KeyGenerator class.

items.title: Java Signature
items.summary: The Java Signature class can create digital signatures from binary data. This Java Signature tutorial explains how to create and verify digital signatures.

items.title: Java Keytool
items.summary: The Java Keytool is a command line tool that can work with Java KeyStore files - e.g. generate key pairs into a KeyStore, import / export certificates etc.

items.title: Java MessageDigest
items.summary: The Java MessageDigest class can calculate a message digest (cryptographic hash value) from a block of data.

items.title: Java Cipher
items.summary: The Java Cipher class represents an encryption algorithm and can encrypt and decrypt data.

items.title: Java KeyStore
items.summary: The Java KeyStore is an encrypted, password protected store for encryption keys.

items.title: Java Cryptography
items.summary: The first part of a new Java Cryptography tutorial has been released!

items.title: Java Regex - Updated
items.summary: The Java Regex (regular expressions) tutorial has been updated to better introduce how regular expressions work.

items.title: Netty ChannelPipeline
items.summary: The Netty ChannelPipeline contains ChannelHandlers that process incoming and outgoing data from sockets.

items.title: Netty TCP client
items.summary: This tutorial explains how to create a TCP client with Netty.

items.title: Netty TCP Server
items.summary: This tutorial explains how to create a TCP server with Netty.

items.title: Netty Overview
items.summary: An overview of Netty for developers getting started with Netty.

items.title: Riot.js Conditionals
items.summary: Riot.js conditionals can show, hide or remove HTML elements depending on the condition specified.

items.title: Riot.js Expressions
items.summary: Riot.js expressions enable you to generate HTML based on JavaScript variables. This tutorial was just updated.

items.title: Riot.js
items.summary: Riot.js is a small JavaScript web framework centered around the ideas of React.js and web components.

items.title: ION Specification
items.summary: ION is a fast, compact, versatile binary data format similar to binary JSON.

items.title: Java GZIPOutputStream Tutorial
items.summary: The Java GZIPOutputStream class can GZIP compress data and write it to an OutputStream.

items.title: Java GZIPInputStream Tutorial
items.summary: The Java GZIPInputStream class can read a GZIP compressed file as a normal InputStream - decompressed.

items.title: ModRun Tutorial
items.summary: ModRun brings modularity to Java without project Jigsaw. Load classes directly from Maven repositories!

items.title: JavaFX StackedAreaChart
items.summary: The JavaFX StackedAreaChart component can stacked draw area charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX AreaChart
items.summary: The JavaFX AreaChart component can draw area charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX LineChart
items.summary: The JavaFX LineChart component can draw line charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX ScatterChart
items.summary: The JavaFX ScatterChart component can draw scatter charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX StackedBarChart
items.summary: The JavaFX StackedBarChart component can draw stacked bar charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX BarChart
items.summary: The JavaFX BarChart component can draw bar charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX PieChart
items.summary: The JavaFX PieChart component can draw pie charts inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: JavaFX TilePane
items.summary: The JavaFX TilePane can layout components in a horizontal or vertical grid of equally sized tiles.

items.title: JavaFX FlowPane
items.summary: The JavaFX FlowPane can layout components in a horizontal or vertical flow, breaking onto next line when there is no more space in the width or height.

items.title: JavaFX Group
items.summary: The JavaFX Group component groups a set of JavaFX components together so effects and transformations can be applied to the components as a group.

items.title: Butterfly DI Container
items.summary: The documentation for Butterfly DI Container has been published on this tutorial website.

items.title: JavaFX TextArea
items.summary: JavaFX TextArea enables the user to enter a text that spans multiple lines.

items.title: JavaFX PasswordField
items.summary: JavaFX PasswordField enables the user to enter a password. The PasswordField hides what is entered - showing circles instead of characters.

items.title: JavaFX DatePicker
items.summary: JavaFX DatePicker enables the user to enter a date or choose a date from a wizard-like popup dialog.

items.title: JavaFX ListView
items.summary: JavaFX ListView enables the user to choose a value from a predefined list of choices.

items.title: JavaFX ComboBox
items.summary: JavaFX ComboBox enables the user to choose a value from a predefined list of choices or type in a value of the user's own choice.

items.title: JavaFX ChoiceBox
items.summary: JavaFX ChoiceBox enables the user to choose a value from a predefined list of choices.

items.title: JavaFX CheckBox
items.summary: JavaFX CheckBox is a button that can be either selected, not selected or in an indeterminate state.

items.title: JavaFX RadioButton
items.summary: JavaFX RadioButton is a button that can be either selected or not selected. As part of a toggle group only one RadioButton in the group can be selected.

items.title: JavaFX ToggleButton
items.summary: JavaFX ToggleButton is a button that can be either pressed or not pressed, and can be part of a toggle group (group of toggle buttons).

items.title: JavaFX MenuButton
items.summary: JavaFX MenuButton can display a list of menu items (options) the user can choose from, like a drop down menu.

items.title: JavaFX TextField
items.summary: JavaFX ImageField enables a user to enter text which can then be read by the JavaFX application.

items.title: JavaFX ImageView
items.summary: JavaFX ImageView can display images inside a JavaFX application. They can also be used inside labels and buttons.

items.title: JavaFX Label
items.summary: JavaFX labels can display text and images inside a JavaFX application.

items.title: Your First JavaFX Application
items.summary: This text how to create a basic JavaFX application, so you can start playing with it.

items.title: JavaFX Overview
items.summary: This text gives an overview of the JavaFX platform and how JavaFX applications are structured.

items.title: Micro Batching
items.summary: Micro batching is a technique that groups tasks into small batches to gain some of the throughput advantages of batching without sacrificing latency too much.

items.title: Java High Performance Read Patterns
items.summary: This tutorial covers 4 common data read patterns in Java and discusses their performance.

items.title: Learning is Hard, But Don't Give Up
items.summary: Learning new topics can often be hard and frustrating. In this article I explain how I face the challenge of learning.

items.title: Standard Deviation
items.summary: The standard deviation of a data set is calculated as the square root of the variance. This standard deviation tutorial explains how to calculate standard deviation of a data set.

items.title: Variance
items.summary: The variance of a data set is a measure of how much the observations in the data set deviate from the mean value of the data set. Variance is also used as base for calculating standard deviation.

items.title: Mean Deviation
items.summary: The mean deviation tells how the observations of a data set are distributed around the mean value of the data set.

items.title: Frequency Distribution
items.summary: The frequency distribution of a data set shows the number of occurrences of each value.

items.title: Range of a Data Set
items.summary: The range of a data set is the difference between the highest and the lowest value. This tutorial explains range with an example.
